title: Green Thumb Industries Q3 2024 Earnings Summary
text: Green Thumb Industries reported Q3 2024 results showing mixed momentum. The company achieved $287 million in revenue, a 4% increase year over year, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $89 million (31% of revenue). Net income totaled $9 million ($0.04 per share). Gross profit stood at $148 million (51% of revenue), while operating expenses reached $105 million. The company generated $48 million in operating cash flow after tax payments of $35 million. The retailer expanded its footprint to 98 locations after opening four new dispensaries, with plans for a 99th store in Orlando. A notable bright spot was consumer packaged goods revenue, which increased over 15% compared to Q3 of last year, driven by market share gains in Ohio, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and New York. The company secured a landmark $150 million syndicated bank loan—described as the cannabis industry's first—enabling it to retire $225 million in senior debt. Capital expenditures totaled $18 million during the quarter ($55 million year-to-date). Management acknowledged ongoing pricing pressure and a deteriorating pricing environment impacting margins. The failed Florida adult-use referendum presented additional headwinds, though the company maintains confidence in its medical market positioning.
